title: The DAO — recursive call drains $60M ETH
note:
A recursive call into `splitDAO` before the balance update bled out the DAO and forced Ethereum's first hard-fork.

## attack vector

**Reentrancy.** `splitDAO` sent ETH to the caller before zeroing their balance, letting an attacker contract re-enter the function and re-split repeatedly while still appearing to hold the original tokens.

## impact

- Funds lost: ~$60M ETH at the time
- Attacker address never publicly attributed

## response

Ethereum hard-forked on block 1,920,000 to roll the funds back. The minority that rejected the rollback became Ethereum Classic (ETC).

title: Parity Multisig — initWallet hijack drains 153k ETH (~$30M)
note:
**Missing access control** on `initWallet` let any address claim ownership of three high-profile multisigs and drain them.

## attack vector

The Parity multisig wallet exposed `initWallet` as a public function with no guard — calling it on an already-initialized wallet overwrote the owner set. Attacker took ownership, then issued normal owner-only withdrawals.

## impact

- Funds lost: 153,037 ETH (~$30M)
- Affected wallets:
  - aeternity — 82,189 ETH
  - Edgeless — 26,793 ETH
  - Swarm City — 44,055 ETH
- Attacker EOA labeled `Multisig Exploit Hacker` on Etherscan.

## response

White-hats (the "White Hat Group") drained remaining vulnerable Parity wallets to safe addresses and returned funds to owners.

title: Parity library suicide bricks ~$300M of ETH permanently
note:
User `devops199` accidentally took ownership of the Parity multisig **library**, then called `kill` — bricking every wallet that delegated to it.

## attack vector

The shared `WalletLibrary` ([`0x863df6bf...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x863df6bfa4469f3ead0be8f9f2aae51c91a907b4)) was uninitialized. `devops199` called `initWallet` on it, became its sole owner, then invoked `kill`, `selfdestruct`-ing the library that 587 deployed multisigs delegated to via `delegatecall`.

## impact

- Funds frozen: ~513,774 ETH (~$300M at the time)
- Affected: 587 multisig wallets, including the Polkadot ICO multisig
- Funds were not stolen — they are unspendable forever.

## response

**EIP-999** proposed restoring the library via state edit. The community vote rejected it. The ETH remains frozen.

title: bZx — first major DeFi flashloan attack ($355k)
note:
The attack that put **flashloans** on the map. A single tx borrowed, manipulated, and arbitraged across four protocols.

## attack vector

Attacker borrowed 10,000 ETH from dYdX, manipulated WBTC/ETH price on Uniswap V1 via a leveraged short opened on bZx Fulcrum, and arbitraged WBTC borrowed from Compound — all atomic in one tx.

## impact

- Net profit: ~1,193 ETH (~$298k)
- Follow-up attack 2020-02-18: another ~$633k
- Attacker funded via Tornado Cash, never attributed

## response

bZx patched and continued operations. The attack pattern became the template for the next year of DeFi exploits.

title: Lendf.Me — imBTC ERC-777 reentrancy drains $25M
note:
**ERC-777 reentrancy.** imBTC's `tokensToSend` callback let the attacker re-enter Lendf.Me mid-withdrawal and inflate their balance recursively.

## attack vector

Lendf.Me called `transfer` before updating internal accounting. imBTC's ERC-777 hook re-entered `supply()` after `withdraw()`, restoring the pre-withdrawal balance — repeated to compounded the bug into an exponential drain.

## impact

- Funds lost: ~$25M across WETH, WBTC, USDx, USDT, USDC, and more
- Victim contract: [`0x0eee3e38...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x0eee3e3828a45f7601d5f54bf49bb01d1a9df5ea) (Lendf.Me)

## response

Attacker was doxxed within days and **returned all funds** within ~96 hours.

title: Harvest Finance — Curve oracle manipulation drains $24M
note:
Flashloan-driven **oracle manipulation** of the Curve y pool inflated `fUSDC`/`fUSDT` mint rates; attacker redeemed for arbitrage profit.

## attack vector

The attacker flashloaned USDC/USDT, swapped large size in the Curve y pool to skew its quoted price, deposited into Harvest's USDC/USDT vaults at a favorable rate, then unwound the swap and redeemed shares for profit. Repeated dozens of times.

## impact

- Funds lost: ~$24M
- Attacker EOA labeled `Harvest.Finance: Hacker 1` on Etherscan
- Funded via Tornado Cash

## response

Harvest offered a $1M bounty for return. ~$2.5M was eventually returned; the rest stayed gone.

title: Pickle Finance — evil jar swap drains $19.7M from cDAI strategy
note:
Unauthenticated jar-swap function let the attacker substitute a fake jar for the real one and walk off with the cDAI behind it.

## attack vector

`ControllerV4.swapExactJarForJar()` lacked **authentication and a Jar whitelist**. Attacker deployed a malicious lookalike Jar, called the swap to move real cDAI from the legitimate `pDAI` jar into theirs, then redeemed for DAI.

## impact

- Funds lost: 19.76M DAI (~$19.7M)
- Funded via Tornado Cash
- No publicly attributed attacker EOA

## response

Pickle paused the controller and patched. Compensated affected users via PICKLE token issuance.

title: Yearn v1 yDAI — Curve 3pool imbalance drains $11M
note:
Flashloan-driven imbalance/rebalance loop on Curve's 3pool forced the v1 yDAI vault to deposit and withdraw at unfavorable rates.

## attack vector

Attacker took an Aave flashloan, repeatedly imbalanced and rebalanced Curve's 3pool, and triggered v1 vault rebalances at each swing — skimming a slice of value on every cycle.

## impact

- Vault losses: ~$11M
- Attacker net profit: ~$2.8M
- Attacker EOA labeled `Yearn (yDai) Exploiter` on Etherscan

## response

Yearn covered user losses from treasury. v1 vaults were deprecated in favor of v2 architecture.

title: Poly Network — cross-chain bridge drained for $611M
note:
Attacker overrode the bridge's keeper public key, then minted unauthorized withdrawals across three chains.

## attack vector

The `EthCrossChainManager` ([`0x838bf9e9...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x838bf9E95CB12Dd76a54C9f9D2E3082EAF928270)) could call into `EthCrossChainData` to update the keeper public key. Attacker crafted a cross-chain message that swapped the keeper for one they controlled, then forged withdrawals from the `LockProxy` / Asset Proxy ([`0x250e7698...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x250e76987d838a75310c34bf422ea9f1ac4cc906)).

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$611M
- Affected chains: Ethereum, BSC, Polygon
- One of the largest exploits in DeFi history at the time

## response

Attacker engaged with the team, claimed white-hat motivation, and **returned the entire $611M** over two weeks. Was offered a $500k bounty and the role of "Chief Security Advisor" — declined.

title: Compound — proposal 062 misallocates $90M+ COMP
note:
Not a hack — a **governance upgrade** introduced a logic bug in the Comptroller that paid out far more COMP than intended.

## attack vector

Proposal 062 ("Distribute COMP to Users") shipped a Comptroller upgrade with a flawed reward calculation. The contract distributed COMP at orders of magnitude beyond intent. No exploit, just opportunistic claimants.

## impact

- Excess COMP distributed: $90M+ at peak
- No single attacker — many recipients refused returns
- Contract: [`0x3d981921...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x3d9819210a31b4961b30ef54be2aed79b9c9cd3b) (Comptroller)

## response

Founder Robert Leshner publicly asked recipients to return funds; some did, some didn't. Patched in subsequent proposal.

title: Cream Finance — yUSDVault price manipulation drains $130M
note:
Third major Cream exploit of 2021. **Manipulated `pricePerShare`** on a yUSDVault by direct-transfer donations, then borrowed against bloated collateral.

## attack vector

yUSDVault's `pricePerShare` derived from raw vault token balances. Attacker direct-transferred tokens to inflate the share price, then deposited a small amount as collateral and borrowed against the inflated value.

## impact

- Funds lost: ~$130M
- Two coordinated attacker EOAs (per rekt.news + ImmuneBytes)
- Cream's third major exploit in 12 months

## response

Cream effectively shut down — never recovered TVL or trust. Merged operations into Yearn ecosystem.

title: BadgerDAO — front-end exploit harvests $120M via injected approvals
note:
Not a smart-contract bug. Attacker injected malicious script into Badger's Cloudflare-served front-end and tricked users into signing unlimited token approvals.

## attack vector

**Front-end compromise** via a stolen Cloudflare API key. The injected script silently mutated user transactions before they hit MetaMask, swapping the spender to the attacker's address. Once approvals were live, the attacker drained vaults at leisure.

## impact

- Funds lost: ~$120M
- Attacker EOA: [`0x1fcdb04d...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x1fcdb04d0c5364fbd92c73ca8af9baa72c269107)
- Attack started 00:00:23 UTC 2021-12-02 — well under the radar of off-hours monitoring

## response

Badger paused contracts and reimbursed affected users from treasury. Cloudflare key rotated; front-end CSP hardened.

title: Wormhole — forged signature mints 120k wETH on Solana
note:
Attacker spoofed a Wormhole guardian signature set on the Solana side, minted 120k unbacked wETH, then bridged the proceeds to Ethereum.

## attack vector

**Signature verification bypass.** The Solana-side bridge accepted a forged guardian signature set as valid, allowing arbitrary mints. 120,000 wETH minted with no underlying ETH lock.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$326M (120k wETH at the time)
- Bridge contract: [`0xf92cd566...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xf92cd566ea4864356c5491c177a430c222d7e678)
- One of the largest bridge hacks ever

## response

**Jump Crypto refilled the bridge** with 120k ETH from its own treasury within ~24 hours, preventing depeg of bridged wETH. Bridge patched and resumed operations.

title: Ronin Bridge — Lazarus phishing drains $625M
note:
**5 of 9 validator keys compromised** via spear-phishing — attacker signed off on fraudulent withdrawals from the Ethereum-side bridge.

## attack vector

Not a smart-contract bug. Sky Mavis engineers were targeted by spear-phishing (fake job offer with a malicious PDF). Once attacker had 5 of 9 validator keys — enough for the bridge's threshold — they forged two large withdrawals from [`0x1a2a1c93...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x1a2a1c938ce3ec39b6d47113c7955baa9dd454f2) and walked.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$625M (173,600 ETH + 25.5M USDC)
- Detection delay: 6 days — discovered only when a user reported a stuck withdrawal
- Attribution: **DPRK Lazarus Group** (US Treasury, Apr 2022)

## response

Sky Mavis raised $150M to repay users. Bridge re-launched with 11 validators (8/11 threshold) and new validator key infra.

title: Inverse Finance — Keep3r oracle manipulation drains $15.6M
note:
Keep3r-fed Anchor oracle manipulated via a large **own-funds swap** (no flashloan), bypassing the TWAP flash-loan guard.

## attack vector

INV/ETH price on SushiSwap fed Anchor's Keep3r TWAP oracle. Attacker used 500 ETH of their own (not flashloaned) to swap INV up, then borrowed ETH/WBTC/DOLA/YFI on Anchor against inflated INV collateral. The non-flashloaned approach sidestepped the TWAP guard, which only checked same-block manipulation.

## impact

- Funds lost: ~$15.6M
- Attacker EOA labeled `Inverse Finance Exploiter 2` on Etherscan
- Funded from Tornado.Cash 2022-04-02 07:22 UTC

## response

Inverse paused Anchor lending and migrated to Chainlink oracles. Treasury covered DOLA bad debt over time.

title: Beanstalk — flashloan governance attack drains $182M
note:
**Flashloaned governance majority.** Attacker borrowed enough stake to pass and instantly execute `emergencyCommit` on a malicious proposal that drained the protocol.

## attack vector

Beanstalk governance let token holders propose and (after a sufficient stake threshold) emergency-commit. Attacker flashloaned hundreds of millions in stable coins, swapped into BEAN3CRV LP, used it to pass + execute their own proposal in a single block — proposal silently transferred reserves to the attacker — then unwound the flashloan.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$182M
- Victim: Beanstalk Farms diamond [`0xc1e088fc...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xc1e088fc1323b20bcbee9bd1b9fc9546db5624c5)
- Attacker net profit: ~$76M (rest covered loan + fees)

## response

Protocol paused. Eventually re-launched with new governance — emergency-execute path removed in favor of timelocked execution.

title: Harmony Horizon Bridge — 2-of-5 multisig compromise drains $100M
note:
Low-threshold multisig — only **2 of 5 signers** required — was compromised. Attacker forged withdrawals from the Ethereum-side bridge.

## attack vector

The Horizon bridge multisig used a 2-of-5 threshold. Two signer machines were compromised (likely targeted phishing). With those two keys, the attacker authorized arbitrary bridge withdrawals from [`0xf9fb1c50...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xf9fb1c508ff49f78b60d3a96dea99fa5d7f3a8a6).

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$100M
- Attacker EOA: [`0x0d043128...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x0d043128146654c7683fbf30ac98d7b2285ded00)
- Attribution: **DPRK Lazarus Group** (US Treasury, Jan 2023)

## response

Harmony proposed a HRM token mint to compensate users — community rejected. Bridge remained broken; ONE token never recovered.

title: Nomad Bridge — copy-paste free-for-all drains $190M
note:
**Init bug** treated unsigned cross-chain messages as valid. Once the first attacker showed the way, hundreds of opportunists copy-pasted the tx with their own address.

## attack vector

A routine upgrade set the trusted root to `0x00`, which the verifier interpreted as "all messages valid". Anyone could call `process()` with a forged message and receive bridge funds. The attack required no coding skill — just changing the recipient address in the calldata.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$190M
- First attacker: [`0x56d8b635...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x56d8b635a7c88fd1104d23d632af40c1c3aac4e3) (`Nomad Bridge Exploiter 1`)
- Hundreds of follow-on copycats — likely the most chaotic single hack in DeFi history
- Victim: Nomad ERC20 Bridge [`0x88a69b4e...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x88a69b4e698a4b090df6cf5bd7b2d47325ad30a3)

## response

Nomad asked white-hats to return funds; ~$30M came back. Bridge eventually shut down.

title: Wintermute — Profanity vanity-address grind drains $160M
note:
Wintermute used a vanity address generated by **Profanity**, a tool with a known guessable seed. Attacker brute-forced the private key.

## attack vector

Profanity used a 32-bit seed for vanity-address generation — exhaustively searchable on consumer GPUs in hours. The vulnerability had been publicly disclosed weeks earlier; Wintermute's [`0x0000000fe6a5...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x0000000fe6a514a32abdcdfcc076c85243de899b) admin/hot wallet was generated with the tool and not rotated.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$160M
- Attacker EOA: [`0xe74b28c2...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xe74b28c2eae8679e3ccc3a94d5d0de83ccb84705) (`Wintermute Exploiter`)
- Compromised wallet: [`0x0000000fe6a5...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x0000000fe6a514a32abdcdfcc076c85243de899b)

## response

Wintermute remained solvent ("twice over") per CEO Evgeny Gaevoy. Vanity addresses generated via Profanity industry-wide were rotated.

title: BNB Chain — IAVL proof forgery mints $586M of BNB
note:
Forged a Merkle proof against a vulnerable **IAVL implementation**, minting 2M BNB cross-chain.

## attack vector

The Token Hub's IAVL Merkle proof verifier had a flaw allowing forged inclusion proofs for messages that were never actually committed. Attacker generated a forged proof, submitted it to the BSC side, and minted 2,000,000 BNB to themselves.

## impact

- BNB minted: 2M (~$586M nominal)
- Funds extracted off-chain: ~$137M (validators paused chain mid-attack)
- Attacker EOA: [`0x489a8756...`](https://bscscan.com/address/0x489a8756c18c0b8b24ec2a2b9ff3d4d447f79bec) (per rekt.news + Nansen + Halborn)

## response

BNB Chain validators **halted the entire chain** mid-attack — the most aggressive containment in any major bridge incident. Patched and resumed within hours.

title: Mango Markets — Avraham Eisenberg oracle manipulation, $114M (Solana, NOT EVM — informational)
note: INFORMATIONAL ONLY — Solana, will be filtered out at seed time. Listed for completeness because it's referenced widely.

title: Euler — donateToReserves liquidation drains $197M
note:
`donateToReserves` let the attacker forfeit collateral while keeping liability — triggering an under-collateralized self-liquidation at a profit.

## attack vector

**Donation accounting bug.** Attacker borrowed against deposited collateral, then called `donateToReserves` to give away their own collateral. The contract reduced their collateral but left their debt intact, putting their account underwater. Attacker then self-liquidated through a controlled liquidator contract, capturing the liquidation discount on phantom debt.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$197M (DAI, WETH, WBTC, USDC)
- Affected: every Euler market
- Victim: Euler [`0x27182842...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x27182842e098f60e3d576794a5bffb0777e025d3)

## response

After months of public negotiation, the attacker **returned 100% of stolen funds**. Euler relaunched as Euler V2.

title: Multichain — MPC keys compromised, bridges drained for $231M
note:
**MPC keys controlled by Multichain's CEO** were compromised after his arrest in China. Funds drained from bridge contracts across six chains.

## attack vector

Multichain's MPC threshold network was — despite branding — heavily centralized in the CEO's control. After his arrest by Chinese authorities, the keys (or the operator running them) drained the bridges. Whether this was state seizure, insider rug, or third-party compromise has never been clarified publicly.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$231M
- Affected chains: Ethereum, Fantom, BSC, Polygon, Arbitrum, Moonbeam
- ~6 attacker EOAs holding loot per rekt.news (per-chain dust addresses, no canonical attacker)

## response

Multichain announced shutdown a few weeks later. Affected protocols (notably Fantom ecosystem) absorbed the loss.

title: Curve — Vyper compiler reentrancy bug drains $73M
note:
**Compiler bug.** Vyper 0.2.15-0.3.0 generated faulty reentrancy locks. Multiple Curve pools drained on the same day.

## attack vector

Vyper's `@nonreentrant` decorator was supposed to prevent reentrancy via a storage flag. In versions 0.2.15-0.3.0, the lock was effectively a no-op for certain function patterns. Pools (`alETH`, `msETH`, `pETH`, `CRV/ETH`) using ETH receive callbacks were re-entered mid-swap.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$73M
- Affected pools: alETH, msETH, pETH, CRV/ETH
- Multiple attackers + MEV front-runners
- [`0x6ec21d18...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x6ec21d1868743a44318c3c259a6d4953f9978538) labeled `Frontrunner (JPEGd_69 Exploit)` — MEV bot that beat the original attacker but kept ~$11M
- [`0xdce5d6b4...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xdce5d6b41c32f578f875efffc0d422c57a75d7d8) is the alETH/ETH pool drainer

## response

White-hat MEV searchers front-ran several attacks; some funds returned. Curve treasury and CRV token absorbed the rest. CRV/USD price crashed; founder Egorov's CRV-collateralized loans came within hours of liquidation cascade.

title: Mixin — cloud DB breach exposes hot wallet keys, $200M drained
note:
Not a smart-contract bug. Mixin's **cloud-service-provider database** was breached, exposing hot-wallet keys.

## attack vector

Mixin's deposit infrastructure stored hot-wallet credentials in a managed database. The cloud provider was breached; attackers exfiltrated keys and drained Mixin's deposit hot wallets across Ethereum and Bitcoin.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$200M (Ethereum + BTC)
- Three attacker EOAs labeled `Mixin Network Exploiter 1/2/3` on Etherscan

## response

Mixin founder publicly guaranteed only **~50% of user assets** post-incident. Operations resumed; users took a haircut.

title: KyberSwap Elastic — concentrated-liquidity tick exploit drains $48M
note:
**Off-by-one in tick boundary handling** let the attacker repeatedly cross the same liquidity range, double-counting liquidity each pass.

## attack vector

KyberSwap Elastic's concentrated-liquidity math had a tick-boundary edge case where a swap could enter and exit a tick range without correctly resetting liquidity state. Attacker swapped back and forth across crafted tick boundaries until pool reserves were drained.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$48M
- Largest single hit: Arbitrum (>$20M, ~42% of total)
- Affected chains: Arbitrum, Optimism, Ethereum, Polygon, Base, Avalanche
- Attacker EOA: [`0x50275e0b...`](https://arbiscan.io/address/0x50275e0b7261559ce1644014d4b78d4aa63be836)

## response

Attacker posted on-chain demands for governance control of Kyber. Negotiation failed. Funds laundered through Tornado Cash. Kyber laid off 50% of staff and shrank operations.

title: PlayDapp — minter role compromise mints 1.79B PLA (~$290M)
note:
**Minter role compromise** on the PLA token contract — attacker minted ~1.79B tokens across two waves and dumped on exchanges.

## attack vector

Attacker added themselves as a minter on the PLA token ([`0x3a4f4063...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x3a4f40631a4f906c2bad353ed06de7a5d3fcb430)), then minted in two waves:

- 200M PLA on Feb 9
- 1.59B PLA on Feb 12

Proceeds were swept onto centralized exchanges for liquidation.

## impact

- Tokens minted: ~1.79B PLA (~$290M nominal)
- Successfully laundered: ~$32M
- Frozen by exchanges: rest
- Two attacker EOAs documented in Nefture / Halborn post-mortems

## response

Exchange coordination froze most proceeds. PlayDapp attempted token migration; PLA price collapsed permanently.

title: Munchables — rogue dev backdoor drains $62M
note:
**Insider attack.** A Munchables team member (suspected DPRK affiliation) inserted a backdoor into the contract during development.

## attack vector

The contract included a custom storage layout under attacker control. After deployment and TVL accumulation, the rogue dev triggered the backdoor and withdrew funds.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$62M
- Rogue dev EOA: [`0x6e8836f0...`](https://blastscan.io/address/0x6e8836f050a315611208a5cd7e228701563d09c5)
- Suspected DPRK affiliation per rekt.news

## response

After community pressure and public negotiation, **funds were returned within ~24 hours** with no losses to users. Highlighted ongoing DPRK infiltration of crypto dev hires.

title: Gala Games — admin key compromise mints 5B GALA (~$200M)
note:
Dormant minter account compromised — attacker minted **5B GALA** on the GALA ERC-20.

## attack vector

A minter account (`idle ~180 days`) on the GALA ERC-20 contract ([`0xd1d2eb1b...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xd1d2eb1b1e90b638588728b4130137d262c87cae)) was compromised — most likely a stale operator key never rotated. Attacker minted 5B GALA and immediately swapped a portion to ETH on DEXes.

## impact

- GALA minted: 5B (~$200M nominal)
- Swapped before block: ~592M GALA → ~5,912 ETH (~$22M)
- Returned to Gala cold wallet: the $22M
- Attacker EOA labeled `Gala Games Exploiter` on Etherscan

## response

Gala **blocklisted the attacker mid-attack** via on-chain admin function. Recovered ~$22M of swapped ETH; rest of the 5B mint was burned/blocklisted.

title: LI.FI — facet upgrade exploit drains $11M from approved users
note:
**Diamond facet upgrade** added an unverified `GasZipFacet` that let the attacker call arbitrary functions on users who'd approved the LiFi diamond.

## attack vector

LiFi diamond ([`0x1231deb6...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x1231deb6f5749ef6ce6943a275a1d3e7486f4eae)) added the `GasZipFacet` without proper validation. The facet exposed an arbitrary-call entry point. For users who held unlimited approvals to the diamond, attacker invoked `transferFrom` indirectly to steal balances.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$11.6M
- Affected: users with unlimited LiFi approvals on Ethereum + Arbitrum
- Attacker EOA: [`0x8b3cb6bf...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x8b3cb6bf982798fba233bca56749e22eec42dcf3)

## response

LiFi disabled the facet, refunded affected users from treasury, and tightened the diamond cut review process.

title: Penpie — fake market reentrancy drains $27M
note:
**Reentrancy via attacker-deployed Pendle market.** `batchHarvestMarketRewards` lacked a reentrancy guard — attacker re-entered `depositMarket` mid-callback.

## attack vector

PendleStaking's `batchHarvestMarketRewards` ([`0xFF51c6b4...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xFF51c6b493c1E4Df4e491865352353EAdff0f9f8)) called into reward tokens before updating reward state. Attacker registered a fake Pendle market with a malicious SY token whose `redeemRewards` callback re-entered `depositMarket`, inflating their reward balance per pass.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$27M across three malicious txs (18:35-18:42 UTC)
- Pendle paused at 18:45 UTC — 3 minutes after last attack tx
- Attacker EOA labeled `Penpiexyz Exploiter` on Etherscan
- Funded ~10 ETH from Tornado.Cash hours before; proceeds laundered back through Tornado

## response

Penpie paused all contracts. Negotiated publicly with attacker for a bounty return; offer rejected. Treasury covered partial reimbursement.

title: DeltaPrime — admin key compromise mints 10^69 dpTokens for ~$6M
note:
Compromised admin key upgraded DeltaPrime Blue's proxies to a malicious implementation, then minted >`1.1e69` dpTokens.

## attack vector

Admin key ([`0x40e4ff9e...`](https://arbiscan.io/address/0x40E4Ff9e018462Ce71Fa34aBdFA27B8C5e2B1AfB)) was compromised. Attacker upgraded the DPUSDC/DPARB/DPBTCb/DPWETH proxies on Arbitrum to a malicious implementation ([`0xD4CA224a...`](https://arbiscan.io/address/0xD4CA224a176A59ed1a346FA86C3e921e01659E73)), minted absurd amounts, and redeemed for the underlying.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$5.98M
- Routed: swapped to ETH, bridged to mainnet, washed through Tornado.Cash
- Attacker EOA: [`0x56e7f672...`](https://arbiscan.io/address/0x56e7f67211683857EE31a1220827cac5cdaa634C)
- DPRK affiliation suspected but not confirmed at disclosure

## response

DeltaPrime paused proxies and migrated. Compensated affected users via treasury + new token.

title: Onyx Protocol — Compound v2 empty-market rounding bug, $3.8M
note:
The **same Compound v2 fork bug** that hit Onyx for $2.1M in November 2023 — exploited again on a freshly listed market via Balancer flashloan.

## attack vector

**Empty-market exchange-rate bug** on low-decimal cTokens: a fresh cToken market with no supply lets the first depositor manipulate the exchange rate via integer-rounding. On the freshly listed `oVUSD` market, attacker flashloaned from Balancer, donated, minted 1 wei of cToken, and inflated the rate to borrow against phantom collateral.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$3.8M
  - 4.1M VUSD
  - 7.35M XCN
  - 5K DAI
  - 0.23 WBTC
  - 50K USDT
- Attacker EOA labeled `OnyxDAO Exploiter 1` on Etherscan
- Attack contract: [`0xAE7d68b1...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xAE7d68b140Ed075E382e0A01d6c67ac675AFa223)

## response

Onyx paused new market listings. The Compound v2 fork class of bug remains a recurring theme — every fork shipping new low-decimal markets without a minimum-supply guard is at risk.

title: Radiant Capital — multisig blind-signing trick drains $50M cross-chain
note:
**3 of 11 multisig signers tricked** by malware that displayed legitimate Gnosis Safe data while routing a malicious upgrade to their hardware wallets.

## attack vector

DPRK-attributed (UNC4736 per Mandiant). Malware on signer machines presented the correct Safe transaction in the browser UI but signed a different transaction on the hardware wallet — a classic **blind-signing UI mismatch**. The malicious tx upgraded `LendingPoolAddressesProvider` to an attacker-controlled implementation.

## impact

- Arbitrum admin multisig ([`0x111CEEee...`](https://arbiscan.io/address/0x111ceeee040739fd91d29c34c33e6b3e112f2177)) executed the upgrade at 17:09:18 UTC
- BSC admin multisig ([`0xE4714D6B...`](https://arbiscan.io/address/0xe4714d6bd9a6c0f6194c1aa8602850b0a1ce1416)) followed at 17:11 UTC
- Funds drained: ~$50M across Arbitrum + BSC
- Malicious contracts also deployed on ETH and Base but never executed

## response

Radiant paused. Attribution to DPRK by Mandiant. Drove industry-wide push for **clear-signing** support on hardware wallets and Safe Transaction Service.

title: Bybit — Safe{Wallet} signing-UI compromise drains $1.43B in ETH/stETH/cmETH/mETH
note:
**Largest crypto theft on record.** DPRK / TraderTraitor (Lazarus) compromised a Safe{Wallet} developer's macOS machine, rode active AWS session tokens past MFA, and pushed a malicious JavaScript payload to `safe.global`'s S3-hosted frontend — scoped to fire only when Bybit's ETH cold wallet signed. One `delegateCall` swapped the Safe's singleton implementation for attacker code; the wallet was drained inside the same transaction.

## attack vector

**Supply-chain attack on the signing UI**, not a smart contract bug. Per the Safe + Mandiant joint forensic, attackers compromised a Safe{Wallet} engineer's macOS development environment, hijacked active AWS session tokens to bypass MFA, and modified the JavaScript bundle in Safe's S3-hosted frontend two days before the attack. The payload was scoped to fire **only** when Bybit's ETH cold wallet (`0x1Db92e2E...`) was the signing context — every other Safe user saw a clean UI. Signers reviewed what looked like a routine transfer in their wallet UIs; the actual signed call was a `delegateCall` to an attacker-controlled contract (`0x96221423...`) that overwrote storage slot 0 of the Safe proxy, swapping the legitimate Safe singleton (`0x34CfAC64...`) for an attacker-deployed singleton (`0xbDd077f6...`) with a hidden `sweepERC20()` function. Full ownership of the cold wallet transferred in one transaction.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$1.43B
  - 401,346 ETH ($1.11B)
  - 90,375 stETH ($250.8M)
  - 15,000 cmETH ($44M)
  - 8,000 mETH ($23.5M)
- Attacker EOA: `0x47666Fab...` (`Bybit Exploiter` per Etherscan, Arkham, Chainalysis)
- Victim Safe (Bybit ETH cold wallet): `0x1Db92e2E...`
- Malicious `delegateCall` target: `0x96221423...`
- Attacker-deployed singleton (new masterCopy): `0xbDd077f6...`
- Attack tx: `0xb61413c4...` (block timestamp 2025-02-21 14:16:11 UTC)
- Laundered via THORChain → BTC
- FBI attribution: **TraderTraitor / Lazarus Group** (IC3 PSA 250226, Feb 26 2025)

## response

Bybit absorbed the loss without halting customer withdrawals — closed the balance-sheet gap via bridge financing within ~72h. Safe{Wallet} **fully rebuilt its infrastructure, rotated all credentials**, and resumed Ethereum mainnet operations via phased rollout per the Mandiant-co-authored post-mortem. Industry-wide review of **multisig signing-UI assumptions** followed: "what you sign" vs. "what you see" cannot rely on a single web frontend rendering the call. Hardware-wallet display verification + parsed-call review on multiple independent UIs became the standard for high-value treasuries.

title: Abracadabra — GM Cauldron solvency bypass via failed GMX orders, $13M
note:
Failed GMX deposits left collateral parked in the OrderAgent while the Cauldron's bookkeeping treated the position as solvent — letting the attacker self-liquidate against phantom collateral.

## attack vector

**Solvency-check desync.** The GM Cauldron asked the OrderAgent for collateral state but didn't account for in-flight failed GMX V2 deposit orders. Attacker engineered a failure path that left collateral in limbo, drove the position underwater, then self-liquidated through a controlled liquidator — collecting the discount on a position whose collateral wasn't really there.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~6,262 ETH (~$13.4M)
- Affected: 5 GM Cauldrons over 56 txs spanning 07:57:52-09:37:36 UTC
- Borrowing paused on all gmCauldrons at 09:46:22 UTC
- Funds bridged Arbitrum → Ethereum via funding/laundering EOA [`0xAF9e33Aa...`](https://arbiscan.io/address/0xAF9e33Aa03CAaa613c3Ba4221f7EA3eE2AC38649) (per rekt.news)

## response

All gmCauldron borrowing paused. Treasury + MIM token absorbed the loss.

title: Cork Protocol — CorkHook missing access control mints fake DS/CT, drains 3,761 wstETH
note:
**`beforeSwap` on CorkHook lacked access control** — a malicious caller passed crafted `hookData` that minted fake derivative tokens against the wstETH:weETH market.

## attack vector

CorkHook's `beforeSwap` accepted arbitrary `hookData` and forwarded it to `CorkCall` without authorization. Attacker passed crafted data that split the protocol's own `weETH8DS-2` tokens into `fake_DS` / `fake_CT`, then redeemed the fakes to drain real wstETH.

## impact

- Funds drained: 3,761.878 wstETH → 4,530 ETH (~$12M) within 16 minutes
- Attacker EOA: [`0xEA6f30e3...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xEA6f30e360192bae715599E15e2F765B49E4da98) (`Cork Protocol Exploiter 1`)
- Protocol had passed **four audits**, including two contests

## response

Cork paused. Public post-mortem highlighted that audits had reviewed individual hook functions in isolation but missed the `beforeSwap` ↔ `CorkCall` interaction.

title: Resupply — ERC-4626 first-donation attack drains $9.6M in fresh vault
note:
Classic **ERC-4626 first-depositor attack** on a `cvcrvUSD` ResupplyPair ([`0x6e90c85a...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x6e90c85a495d54c6d7E1f3400FEF1f6e59f86bd6)) deployed only ~2 hours earlier.

## attack vector

The textbook 4626 inflation attack: into an empty vault, attacker flashloaned $4K USDC from Morpho, donated 2,000 crvUSD, minted 1 wei of shares, and let integer division round the borrow exchange rate to zero. Then deposited 1 wei of `crcrvUSD` as collateral and borrowed ~$10M of `reUSD` against it.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$9.56M
- Routed: swapped to ETH, split across two addresses, washed through Tornado.Cash
- Attacker EOA labeled `Resupply Exploiter 1` on Etherscan

## response

Resupply paused. Industry reminder that **every new ERC-4626 deployment needs a minimum-deposit guard or virtual-shares offset** to immunize against the first-depositor attack.

title: GMX V1 — executeDecreaseOrder reentrancy desyncs GLP, $42M
note:
Reentrancy-like flaw in `PositionManager.executeDecreaseOrder()` on GMX V1 (Arbitrum) let the attacker desync `globalShortAveragePrices` vs. `globalShortSizes` mid-call, inflating GLP price within a single tx.

## attack vector

The vulnerable code path was — ironically — **the patch shipped to fix a 2022 finding**, and went unaudited. The new flow let an attacker re-enter mid-`executeDecreaseOrder` and corrupt the global short-position accounting that backs GLP. Within one tx, GLP price was driven up; attacker minted GLP cheap, redeemed expensive.

## impact

- Funds drained: ~$42M
- Attacker EOA labeled `GMX Exploiter 1` on Arbiscan
- Vault: [`0x489ee077...`](https://arbiscan.io/address/0x489ee077994B6658eAfA855C308275EAd8097C4A)

## response

Attacker took the **white-hat path**: returned ~$37M to the GMX multisig (`0x8D1d2e24...`) and kept a $5M bounty. GMX V1 deprecated; V2 unaffected.

title: Balancer V2 — _upscaleArray rounding error compounds across batchSwap, $128M
note:
**Rounding bug in `_upscaleArray`** combined with crafted `batchSwap` calls drove pool balances into the 8-9 wei range, where Solidity integer division rounded ~10% per swap.

## attack vector

`_upscaleArray` was supposed to normalize pool token balances to 18 decimals. The rounding-down behavior, harmless at normal balances, became catastrophic when balances were driven near zero. Attacker ran 65+ micro-swaps inside the **attack contract's constructor** to keep all state changes atomic, draining V2 boosted pools across six chains in under 30 minutes.

## impact

- Funds drained: $128.64M (WETH/osETH/wstETH from V2 boosted pools)
- Affected chains: Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, Optimism, Avalanche
- Attacker EOA: [`0x506D1f9E...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x506D1f9EFe24f0d47853aDca907EB8d89AE03207) (`Balancer Exploiter 1`)
- Attack contract: [`0x54B53503...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x54B53503c0e2173Df29f8da735fBd45Ee8aBa30d)
- Balancer V3 unaffected

## response

**Berachain halted its network and emergency hard-forked** to contain BEX (Balancer V2 fork) damage — first known emergency hard-fork to contain a DeFi protocol bug at L1 level. Balancer V2 boosted pools deprecated.

title: Resolv — compromised SERVICE_ROLE key mints 80M unbacked USR, $25M extracted
note:
**Off-chain infrastructure compromise**, not a smart-contract bug. Attackers pivoted from a contractor's GitHub credentials into Resolv's AWS KMS environment.

## attack vector

The two-step USR mint flow trusted the `SERVICE_ROLE` signing EOA ([`0x15CAd41e...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x15CAd41e6BdCaDc7121ce65080489C92CF6de398)) to set the minted amount as an **unchecked parameter** via `completeSwap` on the USR Counter ([`0xa27a69Ae...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xa27a69Ae180e202fDe5D38189a3F24Fe24E55861)).

1. Attackers compromised contractor GitHub creds
2. Pivoted into Resolv's AWS KMS
3. Altered KMS access policies to control the SERVICE_ROLE EOA
4. Deposited ~$100K-$300K USDC across three txs, minted ~80M USR via inflated `completeSwap` calls
5. Swapped USR → ~11,400 ETH (~$25M) on Curve before USR depegged

## impact

- USR minted: ~80M (unbacked)
- Extracted: ~$25M ETH
- USR/USDC bottomed at ~$0.025 (17 minutes after first 50M mint)
- First malicious tx [`0xfe37f25e...`](https://etherscan.io/tx/0xfe37f25efd67d0a4da4afe48509b258df48757b97810b28ce4c649658dc33743) at 02:21:35 UTC
- Attacker EOAs: [`0x04A288a7...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x04A288a7789DD6Ade935361a4fB1Ec5db513caEd) (`Resolv Exploiter`), [`0x8ED8cF0C...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x8ED8cF0C1c531C1b20848E78f1CB32fa5B99b81C) (`Resolv Exploiter 2`); [`0xb945eC1b...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0xb945eC1be1f42777F3AA7D683562800B4CDD3890) funded Exploiter 2 with 524 ETH

## response

Resolv paused, **burned hacker-held wstUSR/stUSR balances via contract upgrade**, and published the post-mortem April 4, 2026.

Knock-on impact:
- Euler disabled USR/RLP collateral
- Gauntlet USDC Core had ~$5M wstUSR/USDC exposure

title: Kelp DAO — LayerZero 1-of-1 DVN forged message mints 116,500 unbacked rsETH
note:
Not a smart-contract bug. Attackers compromised LayerZero's DVN RPC nodes and DDoS'd the public ones, forcing the verifier to trust a forged cross-chain message under a **1-of-1 DVN configuration**.

## attack vector

Kelp's bridge relied on LayerZero with a 1-of-1 DVN — a single oracle vote was enough to attest a message. Attackers:

1. Compromised internal RPC nodes feeding the DVN
2. DDoS'd the public RPC backups
3. Forced the verifier to consume only attacker-controlled chain state
4. Submitted a forged cross-chain message that released 116,500 rsETH from Kelp's LayerZero bridge

## impact

- rsETH released: 116,500 (~$292M, ~18% of supply)
- Attack tx: [`0x1ae232da...`](https://etherscan.io/tx/0x1ae232da212c45f35c1525f851e4c41d529bf18af862d9ce9fd40bf709db4222) at 17:35 UTC against LayerZero EndpointV2 ([`0x1a440760...`](https://etherscan.io/address/0x1a44076050125825900e736c501f859c50fE728c))
- Affected chains: Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base, Optimism
- Attribution: **DPRK TraderTraitor / Lazarus** (LayerZero + Chainalysis)

## response

Kelp paused contracts, blocking a follow-up ~$95M theft. Arbitrum Security Council and law enforcement froze ~30K ETH downstream. Attacker laundered via THORChain → BTC. Triggered emergency rsETH freezes across Aave, SparkLend, Fluid, and Upshift.
